title: Supreme Court Declares Right to Walk on Safe Footpaths a Fundamental Right
summary: The Supreme Court of India has ruled that the right to walk on safe footpaths is a fundamental right of citizens. The court directed that all roads must have functional footpaths and called for a law to be enacted to protect pedestrians' right to safe walking infrastructure. The ruling connects pedestrian safety to the right to life and personal liberty under Article 21 of the Constitution. The court's intervention comes amid widespread encroachment of footpaths by vehicles and vendors across Indian cities.
exam_angle: This ruling expands the judicial interpretation of Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty), a recurring theme in GS Paper 2 and Mains essay questions on fundamental rights. It also touches on urban governance and the role of the judiciary in enforcing citizens' rights.
key_facts: Right to walk on safe footpaths declared a Fundamental Right by the Supreme Court
Constitutional basis: Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty)
Court directed all roads to have functional footpaths
Court sought legislation to protect pedestrian rights

title: India-UK Free Trade Agreement: Absence of Investment Chapter Not a Major Concern, Says Expert
summary: A senior expert, Sir Anton Muscatelli, stated that the lack of an investment chapter in the recently concluded India-UK Free Trade Agreement (FTA) is not a significant concern for the deal's overall effectiveness. The India-UK FTA was signed after years of negotiations covering goods, services, and mobility of professionals. The absence of an investment protection chapter means bilateral investment disputes will not be governed under this FTA framework. Separate Bilateral Investment Treaty (BIT) negotiations between India and the UK are expected to address investment protection.
exam_angle: The India-UK FTA is a landmark agreement relevant for GS Paper 2 (International Relations) and GS Paper 3 (Economy/Trade). Questions may focus on FTA structure, investment chapters, and India's trade diplomacy.
key_facts: India-UK FTA concluded after prolonged negotiations
FTA does not include an investment chapter
Bilateral Investment Treaty (BIT) negotiations are separate
Expert: Sir Anton Muscatelli commented on the deal's structure

title: India-US Trade Deal: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal Cites Need for 'Competitive Advantage'; Farmers Protected
summary: Union Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal stated that the India-US bilateral trade deal cannot be implemented until India achieves a 'competitive advantage' in key sectors. Goyal also provided reassurance that Indian farmers would be fully protected under any trade arrangement with the United States. The negotiations are ongoing amid US tariff pressures and India's push for a balanced deal. The Centre has maintained that agriculture will not be compromised in trade negotiations.
exam_angle: India-US trade negotiations are critical for GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy). The protection of Indian agriculture in trade deals is a recurring policy concern connecting to food security and WTO commitments.
key_facts: Minister: Piyush Goyal (Commerce & Industry)
India insists on 'competitive advantage' before implementing US trade deal
Indian farmers assured of full protection in trade negotiations
India-US bilateral trade deal negotiations are ongoing
source: The Economic Times / Times of India / NDTV

title: Iran Negotiators Depart for Switzerland to Hold Peace Talks with the United States
summary: Iranian negotiators have left for Switzerland to engage in diplomatic discussions with the United States aimed at reaching a peace or nuclear deal. The talks are part of renewed diplomatic efforts between Washington and Tehran following escalating regional tensions. Switzerland serves as a neutral diplomatic venue given the absence of formal US-Iran diplomatic relations. These negotiations are significant in the context of Iran's nuclear programme and global non-proliferation efforts.
exam_angle: Iran-US nuclear negotiations are relevant for GS Paper 2 (International Relations), particularly topics like nuclear non-proliferation, the JCPOA framework, and India's stakes in West Asian stability and energy imports.
key_facts: Iran negotiators travelled to Switzerland for US-Iran peace/nuclear talks
Switzerland hosts talks due to its neutral status and lack of US-Iran formal diplomatic ties
Talks relate to Iran's nuclear programme and regional peace
Context: Renewed US-Iran diplomatic engagement

title: Nobel Laureate John Jumper Leaves Google DeepMind to Join Anthropic
summary: John Jumper, winner of the 2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry for his work on AlphaFold protein structure prediction at Google DeepMind, has resigned to join Anthropic, an AI safety-focused startup. Jumper was a key scientist behind AlphaFold 2, which revolutionised structural biology by predicting protein 3D structures with near-experimental accuracy. Anthropic, founded by former OpenAI researchers, develops large language models including Claude. This move highlights the intense talent competition in the global AI industry.
exam_angle: AlphaFold and protein structure prediction represent a landmark intersection of AI and biology relevant to GS Paper 3 (Science & Technology). Nobel Prize awardees and their contributions are standard 'Persons in News' topics.
key_facts: John Jumper: 2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry (co-recipient) for AlphaFold
AlphaFold developed at Google DeepMind — predicts protein 3D structures using AI
Jumper has now joined Anthropic (AI safety company, developer of Claude AI)
AlphaFold 2 considered a breakthrough in structural biology

title: Karnataka Government Appoints Kunal Vajani and Omar Hoda as Additional Advocate Generals in Supreme Court
summary: The Karnataka state government has appointed Kunal Vajani and Omar Hoda as Additional Advocate Generals to represent the state in the Supreme Court of India. Advocate Generals are appointed by state governments under Article 165 of the Constitution to provide legal advice and represent the state in courts. Additional Advocate Generals assist in handling the state's growing legal caseload in the apex court. This appointment is part of Karnataka's legal representation strategy.
exam_angle: The office of Advocate General is established under Article 165 of the Constitution, making this relevant for GS Paper 2 (Polity — State Executive). Appointments to constitutional offices are standard exam topics.
key_facts: Appointees: Kunal Vajani and Omar Hoda as Additional Advocate Generals
Appointed by: Karnataka state government
Constitutional provision: Article 165 (Advocate General for the State)
Role: Legal representation of Karnataka in the Supreme Court
